statement.p: To convert a temperature in degrees Celsius to degrees Kelvin, there is a formula K = C + 273\text{.} The surface temperature of Pluto is about 40 Kelvin. What is the surface temperature of Pluto in degrees Celsius?

solution.p: Since we know the Kelvin temperature is 40\text{,} the formula becomes the equation
and we need to solve for C\text{.} With 273 being added, we should subtract 273 from each side.
We should check the solution by substituting -233 in for C in the original equation:
This checks out, so the surface temperature of Pluto is -233 degrees Celsius.

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(A.7.17) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "PCCmacros.pl", "parserNumberWithUnits.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context('Numeric'); $answer = NumberWithUnits("-233 degC");
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML To convert a temperature in degrees Celsius to degrees Kelvin, there is a formula [`K = C + 273\text{.}`] The surface temperature of Pluto is about [`40`] Kelvin. What is the surface temperature of Pluto in degrees Celsius?
[@KeyboardInstructions('You may enter [|degC|]* for ^\{\\circ\}C.')@]**
[_]{$answer}{10}
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION Since we know the Kelvin temperature is [`40\text{,}`] the formula becomes the equation
[``` 40 = C + 273 ```]
and we need to solve for [`C\text{.}`] With [`273`] being added, we should subtract [`273`] from each side.
[```\newcommand{\subtractright}[1]{{\highlight{{{}-#1}}}}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} 40\subtractright{273}\amp= C + 273\subtractright{273}\\ -233\amp=C \end{aligned} ```]
We should check the solution by substituting [`-233`] in for [`C`] in the original equation:
[```\newcommand{\substitute}[1]{{\color{blue}{{#1}}}}\newcommand{\confirm}[1]{\stackrel{\checkmark}{#1}}\newcommand{\wonder}[1]{\stackrel{\text{?}}{#1}}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} 40\amp=C + 273\\ 40\amp\wonder{=}\substitute{-233} + 273\\ 40\amp\confirm{=}40 \end{aligned} ```]
This checks out, so the surface temperature of Pluto is [`-233`] degrees Celsius.
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
